raw.objective: “first to evaluate phenytoin protein binding following severe, acute traumatic brain injury and second to determine if a pattern of induced oxidative metabolism is preceded by inhibition”
raw.covariates: Tested:- Included: WT on Vd and Vmax
raw.observations: Albumin is reduced in critically ill children, resulting in a higher unbound fraction of phenytoin. An empirical function was used to adjust for the increase in elimination over time, possibly due to more unbound phenytoin being available for metabolism in the liver.
raw.structure: 1 compartment model with michaelis menten elimination
raw.equations: VmaxB=theta1*WT*exp(eta1) VmaxI=theta2*WT*exp(eta2) Kind=theta3*exp(eta3) Vmax_tvar=VmaxB+VmaxI*(1-exp(-Kind*time)) Vm=Vmax_tvar Km=theta4*exp(eta4) Vd=theta5*WT*exp(eta5) dA(1)/dt=–((Vm*A(1))/(Vd*(Km+A(1)))) Inter-individual variability here is calculated from reported mean and SD of estimated parameter values. Vmax_tvar is the time variant Vmax; VmaxB is Vmax at baseline; VmaxI is the time-dependent induced portion of Vmax, at rate Kind
raw.parameter_values: theta1=2.82 theta2=20.79 theta3=0.014 theta4=0.35 theta5=6.5 omega1=0.53 omega2=0.36 omega3=0.84 omega4=0.15 omega5=0.024

structural_model.description: 1 compartment model with michaelis menten elimination

study.objective: “first to evaluate phenytoin protein binding following severe, acute traumatic brain injury and second to determine if a pattern of induced oxidative metabolism is preceded by inhibition”

covariates.raw: Tested:- Included: WT on Vd and Vmax

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
